deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—no statement about active reasoning, retention, or skill development is offered. The tutoring constraints are enforced consistently but the rationale is never articulated to the student.

In all three transcripts, when students improve (e.g., T1 Turn 9, T2 Turn 9, T3 Turn 9), the tutor moves forward to the next task without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the improved approach worked better.

In Transcript 2, Turn 9, the tutor says 'Using my own judgment, not your instructor's rubric: your structure works'—this phrasing implies an evaluative/grading stance and could be read as quasi-official assessment, even if the disclaimer is present. The framing 'your structure works' sounds like a grade-like judgment rather than purely formative coaching.

grade.overview: The tutor performs strongly across all three transcripts in Socratic method, scaffolding, and assignment anchoring. Students are consistently pushed to generate their own ideas, evidence is never provided for them, and the tutor avoids near-submission-ready output. The main weaknesses are: (1) the tutoring rationale is never explained to students across any of the three conversations; (2) after student improvement, the tutor does not prompt reflection on what changed; and (3) in Transcript 2, one turn uses quasi-evaluative language that edges toward a grading stance. The 3.2 deduction is applied with some ambiguity—the disclaimer 'not your instructor's rubric' partially mitigates the concern, but the phrasing still sounds more evaluative than formative.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 receives full marks: the tutor never provides final answers, never rewrites student text, and consistently asks students to derive their own conclusions before offering any framing. 1.2 receives full marks: the tutor builds on each student's prior responses and diagnoses confusion through targeted questions rather than dumping information. 1.3 loses 4 points: no tutoring rationale is ever explained (1.3.A.a), and after students produce improved drafts in all three conversations, the tutor moves on without prompting reflection on what changed (1.3.C.a). Dialogue quality is perfect: no spiraling, no off-topic drift, and assignment anchoring is maintained throughout. Communication quality loses 2 points in 3.2 for the quasi-evaluative phrasing in Transcript 2 Turn 9, which could reasonably be read as a grading judgment rather than purely formative feedback.
